description:
Credibility gap: a mutation tester that reports no mutation score on its own code. `self_mutation_refused` (.spec/decisions/self_mutation_refused.md) makes in-process self-mutation impossible (the runner would crash mutating itself), so this needs a child-BEAM / external-process approach — essentially the test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs pattern aimed at mutagen_ex itself.

Produce a mutation score for the high-value modules (mutators/*, scope_resolver, cli, json_reporter) and publish it.

Larger effort than the other children — fine to land after the initial publish, but high-value for credibility.

Acceptance: a reproducible self-mutation score exists and is cited in the README/docs.

comments.text: ORCHESTRATOR NOTE: first implementer attempt was interrupted by a Claude spend/usage limit before it could commit or post IMPLEMENTED (infra, not a code defect). Its uncommitted scratch (a self_mutation_test.exs + self_mutation_harness.exs sketch) was saved for reference. Stage reset to the current epic HEAD (004f219, which now contains the .1/.2/.4 README sections) and a fresh --impl-only worker is being respawned on the remembers profile. This consumes attempts.impl (now 2 of 2).

title: Dogfood: child-BEAM self-mutation score
